calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-18-2018 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-04-2017 Set For Trial On Jun-11-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-18-2017 Continued To Oct-18-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-31-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Strouss III (415-771-6174) for Plaintiff/opposing party .
William McCaslin, Esq. (707-427-3998) of McNamara, Ney, Beatty, SLattery , Borges for Defendant/ Moving party ANDREW THORNTON through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues intoxication. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcripts) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANDREW THORNTON's MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES

Defendant Andrew Thornton's motion to strike plaintiff's request for punitive damages is denied. Plaintiff Larenzo Bell adequately alleges that, by knowingly driving while intoxicated, Mr. Thornton acted despicably and with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights of others within the meaning of Civil Code 3294(c)(1), the definition of malice for purposes of punitive damages liability. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcripts)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR#11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-07-2017 Continued To Sep-06-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
